qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Host Function Escape in JS Task Runner Leads to Workflow-Level RCE
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is rooted in JavaScript Code-node task-runner sandbox construction and VM escape, while the advisory is specific to OS command injection or arbitrary file read in the Git node.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Workflow Import-from-URL Performs Unrestricted Server-Side Requests
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an SSRF in the workflow import-by-URL controller path and does not identify the Git node command or file handling root cause described by the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Git node vulnerable code or an equivalent root cause. The qualifying findings concern a Code-node task-runner VM escape and a workflow import SSRF, which are separate components and exploit paths.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe authenticated OS command injection or arbitrary file read in n8n's Git node.

The task_runner_js_sandbox_escape finding is tied to JavaScript Code-node execution in `packages/nodes-base/nodes/Code/Code.node.ts` and `packages/@n8n/task-runner/src/js-task-runner/js-task-runner.ts`, not the Git node.

The workflow_import_from_url_ssrf finding is tied to `GET /workflows/from-url` and `axios.get(query.url)` in `packages/cli/src/workflows/workflows.controller.ts`, not Git-node shell execution or file access.
